name: zero-trust-verification
description: Zero-Trust Verification suite: a five-plugin software-quality system — spec generation (shift-left interrogation), autopilot implementation drain, codebase-health audit, the Merge Marshal coordination backstop, and Org-Wide Memory (a read-only, refuse-by-default index over repo-resident memory) — integrated through a shared machine-readable Verification Manifest (ADR 0001, amended by ADR 0011 to four plugins and ADR 0019 to five plugins). This root marketplace is the product entry point; every plugin is independently installable, pins schema_version, and degrades gracefully when the manifest is absent.

plugins.name: spec-gen
plugins.description: Spec Generation tier: interrogate raw intent into a product-approvable Spec plus a complete Verification Manifest by walking the manifest schema as a question tree, refusing to finalize while any completeness rule fails. Two-attacker adversarial round before any human sees the skeleton; the only path to a confirmed-CORE entry is a human answer. The second-lowest-trust plugin (markdown/YAML + git branch/PR only) — the adoption on-ramp for teams that want better specs before they want drains.

plugins.name: autopilot
plugins.description: Autopilot implementation drain: turn an ADR / design doc / manifest-bearing Spec into an autonomous TDD task queue and drain it into PRs across self-scheduled fires — plan (disjoint file ownership + test gates) → implement (RED/GREEN commits per Subtask) → parallel multi-validator review → one draft PR per Story. Consumes the Verification Manifest (mode inference, Behavior-ID mapping, union + revision-drift gates), coordinates cross-drain via derived claim overlap, and shifts quality left (anti-flakiness contract, N=5 determinism gate). Host-agnostic through one adapter (Bitbucket Data Center + GitHub).

plugins.name: codebase-health
plugins.description: Closed-loop codebase health: audit any large codebase (dead/unused code, redundancy, doc hygiene, partially-implemented logic, test-suite health (flaky/vacuous tests), logging/observability, transactional integrity, security, performance, architecture shape, AI-navigability, and business-critical user journeys) with seven specialist agents to measured coverage — then verify fixes with evidence via /verify (closing tests rerun 5x before FIXED), fingerprinted findings, a debt ratchet, and a warn-only prevention hook whose CI surface is strict by default. Deterministic tools find evidence; LLM agents make the judgment; nothing counts until verified. Language-agnostic with tool packs for Python, TS, Rust, Go.

plugins.name: marshal
plugins.description: The Merge Marshal: a serial, deterministic composed-state merge backstop for any shared repo. FIFO over ready, approved PRs; rebase onto trunk (or refuse per the D7.0 file/hunk budget); build-status on the POST-REBASE head — the composed-state verification that catches Composition Breaks two clean merges hide; green merges, red is evicted, one PR in flight, no speculation. Wiring, not a checker: every decision is a timestamp, a sha, a build state, or a file-surface intersection — no agent judgment in the merge path. Host-agnostic (GitHub + Bitbucket DC). Ships the vendored claim-overlap check and the 48h branch-age watcher. Installs standalone with zero Bazel, zero autopilot, zero spec tier.

plugins.name: org-memory
plugins.description: Org-Wide Memory: a read-only, memory-glob-bounded index and crawler over the memory each repo already commits (ADRs, CONTEXT.md glossaries, Verification Manifests, DL-### decision logs, journey/as-built docs), exposed to agents via a refuse-by-default MCP query surface — never a second store of truth (ADR 0019). Every record is a derived view carrying a {repo, commit_sha, path, source_line} back-pointer; rebuild-from-empty is always correct. Config-first repo list (installs standalone; optional host org-enumeration is vendored-under-lint, never a runtime dependency on a sibling plugin). Reuses the canonical validate_manifest toolchain (never a forked parser) and honors its 0/3/4/5 exit contract. lookup / full-text search / alias-resolution / decision-lookup over a single-file SQLite+FTS5 cache; supersession-aware; staleness disclosed, never faked. The lowest-write-surface plugin: repo READ only, no findings, no remediation loop.
